Name: The Great Sumerian Deluge
Effect: Put this aside at the start of the game. The first time there are 8 or more humans in play, the flood happens at the end of that turn. The Flood: Banish all humans.
Anecdote: 'The noise of mankind has become too much. I am disturbed by their uproar. Sleep no longer comes to me.' — Enlil, before the council

Name: Cuneiform Tablets of Ea
Effect: On enter: Search your deck for 'The Ark' and add it to your hand. While on top: You may discard a card to look at the top 3 cards of your deck and arrange them in any order.
Anecdote: 'Take the design of the ship I draw for you on the ground! Let its dimensions be measured exactly...'

Name: Flies
Effect: If a being left the world this turn, you may play this for free.
Anecdote: They come where something has ended. They are never far behind.

Name: Slave
Effect: While on top: You may banish this to play your next artefact this turn for [2] less.
Anecdote: His hands know the weight of stone and brick. He has built the city. He will not be remembered in it.

Name: Shepherd
Effect: On enter: The next human you play costs [1] less.
Anecdote: Where he walks, others follow. He does not ask them to.

Name: Farmer
Effect: On enter: Play another 1 cost human for free.
Anecdote: The field is never worked alone. One calls to his neighbor, and his neighbor comes.

Name: Fisherman
Effect: On enter: Draw two humans from your underworld.
Anecdote: He casts his net into the dark water and pulls out what the deep sees fit to return.

Name: Sacrificer at the Altar
Effect: On enter: If the flood would happen at the end of this turn, it happens at the end of your next turn instead.
Anecdote: The gods are not easily turned. But they can be made to wait.

Name: Weeping Mother Goddess
Effect: On enter: Choose one of your humans with a cost of [2] or less and return it to its owner's hand.
Anecdote: 'How could I have agreed to destroy my own children in the council of gods?' She would not be consoled.

Name: Shipbuilder
Effect: While on top: Artifacts you play cost [1] less.
Anecdote: He has never asked what the ship is for. He asks only whether the joints will hold.

Name: Citizen of Shruppak
Effect: On enter: Draw two humans.
Anecdote: Shruppak was a city that knew the gods. Its streets were full, and its walls were old. It did not think the world could end.

Name: Elders of Shuruppak
Effect: While on top: Double the power of all your humans here.
Anecdote: They have seen five kings come and go. When they speak, even the young men are quiet.

Name: Atrahasis, Flood Survivor
Effect: Immortal.
Anecdote: Ea leaned close to the wall of reeds and spoke just loud enough for one man to hear. That was all it took.

Name: Enlil, Storm God
Effect: On enter: If there are two or more humans here, you may unleash the flood at this location.
Anecdote: He did not hate them. He simply could not sleep.

Name: The Ark
Effect: Costs [1] less for each of your humans. Indestructible. On enter: Choose a location. While on top: Your humans on chosen location are protected from the flood.
Anecdote: Six decks, seven compartments, nine sections. Pitch sealed every seam. Inside: gold, silver, all living things, his kin, the craftsmen, the cattle of the field. The rain began at dawn.

Name: Eurybates, Herald of Odysseus
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Odysseus'.
Anecdote: 'Of all his heralds, Odysseus valued Eurybates most — round-shouldered, dark, and sharper than he looked.'

Name: Calchas, Prophet of Apollo
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck. Put one into your hand and the other at the bottom.
Anecdote: 'He alone could read the flight of birds, and told the Greeks what the gods demanded before the fleet could sail.'

Name: Sinon the Deceiver
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. While on top: The opponent must play with their hand revealed.
Anecdote: 'He stood before the Trojans, hands bound, and told them exactly enough of the truth to be believed.'

Name: Greek Soldiers
Effect: When Greek Soldiers move to the opponent's side of this location: For that opponent, you may destroy up to five of their beings with Power [1] or less.
Anecdote: 'In the darkness of the horse's belly we waited, crouched, armed, silent. Sinon's torch was the signal.'

Name: Dolon the Scout
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. While on top: Once per turn you may look at the top card of the opponent's deck and choose to put it on the bottom.
Anecdote: 'He wore the wolf-skin and went out alone into the dark, certain no one would catch him. He was wrong.'

Name: Menelaus, the Wronged King
Effect: While on top: This card has +2 Power for each card the opponent controls more than you at this location.
Anecdote: 'Ten years. Not for glory. Not for Agamemnon's pride. He simply refused to be the man whose wife left and who did nothing.'

Name: Camp Guard at the Ships
Effect: On enter: Draw 1 card. If you control a card on the opponent's side of the location, draw 2 cards instead.
Anecdote: 'His orders were simple: watch the plain, and shout if the fires moved.'

Name: Epeius, Builder of the Horse
Effect: On enter: Draw 'The Trojan Horse'.
Anecdote: 'Athena herself guided his hands. He wept as he built it, knowing what it was for.'

Name: Odysseus
Effect: On enter: Move a card here to any location at any side.
Anecdote: 'He is never at a loss. That is what makes him dangerous.'

Name: Patroclus
Effect: On enter and if Achilles is in play: For each opponent destroy an enemy Being here with Power equal to or less than Patroclus.
Anecdote: 'He put on Achilles' armour, and the Trojans broke before him — until they didn't.'

Name: The Trojan Horse
Effect: When this card moves to the opponent's side of the location: You may move any number of your Humans with it. Flip them face-down; they have −6 Power.
Anecdote: 'They brought it inside themselves. That was always the plan.'

Name: Ajax, the Great
Effect: While on top: Your Beings here cannot be moved by opponent card effects.
Anecdote: 'He gave ground for nothing and no one. When the others fled to the ships, Ajax walked backwards, facing the enemy.'

Name: Agamemnon, King of Mycenae
Effect: While on top: The opponent can have a maximum of 3 cards on their side of this location.
Anecdote: 'He was not the greatest warrior, nor the wisest. He was simply the one no one could ignore — and he never let them forget it.'

Name: Achilles
Effect: On enter: For each opponent, destroy an enemy Being here with Power equal to or less than Achilles. If Patroclus is in the underworld: For each opponent, destroy the strongest enemy Being here instead.
Anecdote: 'He knew he would not return. He chose to come anyway.'

Name: Diomedes, the God-Smiter
Effect: While on top: For each opponent, their strongest deity's power here is counted as 0.
Anecdote: 'He drove his spear into Ares, and the war god screamed. Even gods can bleed.'
